title: Functional and structural adaptations following immersive audiovisual training in post-stroke hemianopia
why: Closest direct match to NegletFix: 6 weeks of immersive AV training in post-stroke HH, n=15, improved reaction time with DTI/FC changes.
next_action: Keep as current core evidence anchor beside Rowland 2023 and Alharshan/Alwashmi protocol notes.

title: Assessment and Rehabilitation of Post-Stroke Visual Field Impairments: Current Approaches and Emerging Technologies
why: Fresh narrative review covering perimetry, substitution, eye-movement therapy, restitution, brain stimulation, and VR/wearables.
next_action: Use as Sprint 4 umbrella source for roadmap language.

title: Screening and Rehabilitation of Visual Field Defects in Post-Stroke Patients
why: Not-yet-recruiting 2026 study of bedside visual-field screening plus early multisensory AV stimulation training.
next_action: Monitor for protocol details and results.

title: Neuromodulation Through Multisensory Stimulation for Visual Field Deficits in the Subacute Stage of Disease
why: Recruiting 2026-2029 trial combining tDCS with audio-visual training for subacute visual field deficits after stroke.
next_action: Monitor as the best active AV+tDCS clinical-trial lead.

title: Reinforcement-Based Head-Scan Training for Drivers with Homonymous Visual Field Loss Improves Blind-Side Detection
source: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation 2026
why: Fresh Bowers Lab single-arm simulator study: three 1-hour sessions improved proactive blind-side scanning and hazard detection.
next_action: Use for VR functional-measurement ideas only; not driving clearance.

title: A randomized controlled trial of Scanning Eye trAining as a Rehabilitation Choice for Hemianopia after stroke (SEARCH)
why: Large RCT found both VST and sham groups improved but no significant between-group difference.
next_action: Keep as major caution against overclaiming generic scanning therapy.

title: Enhancing multisensory rehabilitation of visual field defects with transcranial direct current stimulation
why: Proof-of-principle RCT: audiovisual compensatory training with anodal tDCS in chronic HVFDs.
next_action: Clinician/research-only branch; do not alter current open-loop AV protocol yet.

title: Boosting hemianopia recovery: the power of interareal cross-frequency brain stimulation
why: Double-blind crossover cf-tACS + perceptual training study reports boosted motion discrimination and isopter changes.
next_action: Watch as advanced lab stimulation branch, not home protocol.

title: PAVE system for chronic visual field loss
why: Recruiting 2026 VR headset treatment for chronic visual field loss due to stroke/TBI/brain surgery.
next_action: Commercial/device branch; watch outcomes but do not mix into NegletFix claims.

title: Statistical Learning as a Novel Intervention for Cortical Blindness
why: University of Rochester invitation trial for cortical blindness / HH / occipital stroke using visual training and learning tasks.
next_action: Watch Huxlin/Cavanaugh line for personalized visual learning ideas.

title: Functional connectivity interacts with visual perceptual learning for visual field recovery in chronic stroke
why: Suggests baseline visual-network connectivity may predict VPL response after chronic stroke.
next_action: Relevant to future personalization; not needed for current implementation.

title: Exogenous Spatial Attention Helps Overcome Spatial Specificity of Visual Learning in the Blind Field After V1 Damage
why: Pre-cues helped transfer visual learning deeper into blind field in some cortical-blindness participants.
next_action: Useful design clue for cueing and attentional manipulation.

title: Persistence of training-induced visual improvements after occipital stroke
why: Extended training gains persisted in some but not all participants; heterogeneity remained important.
next_action: Supports long-term follow-up and responder/non-responder tracking.

title: Measuring residual visual function after cerebral damage
why: Argues fMRI field maps may reveal spared cortex missed by perimetry/anatomy alone.
next_action: Future advanced clinical mapping branch.

title: Technological visuo-cognitive training after stroke: pilot study
why: Small pilot using Senaptec station showed functional visual-search/RT changes but sensory measures unchanged.
next_action: Useful for technology usability and functional-measurement framing.

title: The Hemianopia Reading Questionnaire
why: Developed HRQ for self-reported reading difficulties in homonymous visual field defects.
next_action: Potential source for improving Sprint 3 reading tracker.

title: Occupational Therapists' perspectives of implementing HABIT for visual field loss due to stroke
why: Implementation study highlights time, accessibility, text difficulty, and patient awareness barriers in real stroke services.
next_action: Useful for making NegletFix outputs simpler and more accessible.

title: Clinical Trial of Multi-Periscopic Prism Glasses for Hemianopia
why: Active-not-recruiting prism trial comparing multi-periscopic and Fresnel peripheral prism glasses.
next_action: Clinician/accommodation branch; not restorative AV core.

title: Bowers Laboratory
why: Active hemianopia/quadrantanopia driving-simulator and vision studies; Alex Bowers is a recurring source.
next_action: Monthly search author/lab name plus PubMed.

title: Professor Fiona Rowe research page
why: SEARCH/HABIT/VISION/IVIS ecosystem; direct stroke-vision service and research relevance.
next_action: Track papers, trial results, and patient-facing resources.

title: Huxlin Lab / VisPeR Lab
why: Active cortical blindness / occipital stroke visual restoration group with clinical trials and VPL work.
next_action: Track Huxlin/Cavanaugh/Willis/Raffin publications and trials.
